summary: Great‑sounding Miller soundboard records an uneven 1971 concert with a standout Hard to Handle and Morning Dew, but a weaker first set and shaky encores.
review: The 24‑bit Miller soundboard source is praised for its pitch‑accurate, full‑range clarity, though the older 16‑bit cut has a noticeable edit. Musically the show sits just above average for the era – the second set shines with Hard to Handle and Morning Dew while the first set feels only B‑ level and the triple encore runs thin.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is around average (≈3/5) with a few high points; recording quality is good to excellent (≈4/5). Weighted combination yields ~3.3 stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: 24‑bit Miller source described as great‑sounding and pitch‑accurate; 16‑bit version has a cut and misses a post‑Playin' shout, but overall clarity and completeness are high.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A long, 2+‑hour second set with a triple encore; the first set is solid but uneven (B‑), while the second set (C+) lifts the show despite weaker encores.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Snarly tone on his first Alembic custom guitar noted on Bertha and possibly steel‑sitting on Dark Hollow; overall guitar work praised on Hard to Handle.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned wanting a different bass approach during Sugar Magnolia; no specific performance praise or criticism.
band_member_comments.Drums: Jam between Drums and GDtRFB described as ahead of its time, sounding like ’73 and briefly becoming Stephen.
